concerns.text: Phase 8 tasks (T060-T065) address reviewer requests for experimental validation and physical constraint verification that directly contradict spec.md's explicit out-of-scope declarations. T061 validates bond lengths against physical constants despite spec.md stating 'Physical measurement validation is out of scope'. T062 documents hydration state error margins despite spec.md stating 'hydration effects are out-of-scope'. Tasks cannot override spec scope boundaries.

Task file paths use 'projects/001-predicting-molecular-dipole-moments/' while constitution.md and plan.md use 'projects/PROJ-262-predicting-molecular-dipole-moments-with/'. Constitution Principle V (Versioning Discipline) requires consistent project ID across all artifacts. This creates directory structure ambiguity and violates FR-030.

T032 validates predictions against QM9 DFT reference data, but QM9 IS the training data source (FR-001). This creates circular validation rather than independent verification. FR-011 states 'validate predictions against QM9 quantum calculation reference data' but the same dataset cannot serve as both training source and independent validation ground truth.

T063 implements SHAP/Integrated Gradients for interpretability beyond spec FR-007's specified methods (permutation importance + saliency mapping only). FR-007 explicitly states '2 methods total'. Adding SHAP/Integrated Gradients exceeds the functional requirement scope without spec amendment.

T051 validates RMSE variance < 10% as a separate task, but SC-005 requires this metric to be measured during training (T028/T029). This creates redundant validation work. The variance check should be integrated into training tasks, not post-hoc validation.

Both T034 (metrics.csv generation) and T044 (confidence interval computation) handle 95% CI for MAE/RMSE with unclear ownership. T034 generates metrics.csv with CI columns while T044 computes confidence intervals. Responsibility should be consolidated to avoid duplication.

T053 validates literature URLs exist, but Constitution Principle II requires citation verification against primary sources with title-token-overlap ≥ 0.7 threshold. URL existence validation alone does not satisfy the Verified Accuracy gate requirement.
